name: Cabinet
description: A lightweight self-hosted file cabinet and document manager.
summary: Cabinet is an AI-first, self-hosted knowledge base and 'startup OS' built on file-based markdown storage with no database. The latest release is v0.3.4 (Apr 14, 2026), part of a series of 15 releases. Key recent additions include a structured AI provider adapter layer (supporting Claude Code CLI and Codex CLI), per-run model/provider overrides, embedded HTML app rendering inside the knowledge base, and a web terminal for interactive CLI sessions. The project is MIT-licensed, open source, and maintained by Hila Shmuel with an active community on Discord.

answers.Is the project actively maintained?: Yes — v0.3.4 released Apr 14, 2026, with 15 releases and 319 commits
answers.Does it support user authentication?: Optional — set KB_PASSWORD env variable; empty means no auth
answers.Is there a Docker image available?: A .dockerignore file is present suggesting Docker support, but no official image is explicitly documented in the changelog

breaking_excerpts: See CHANGELOG.md for breaking changes (referenced but not fully reproduced here)
AI runtime no longer treats the browser terminal as the only way to run AI work — tasks, jobs, and heartbeats now run through a provider adapter layer, which may break existing integrations
Default AI adapters changed to structured local adapters: claude_local for Claude Code and codex_local for Codex CLI

alternatives.name: Paperless-ngx
alternatives.review: Paperless-ngx offers far more advanced document management with OCR, tagging, and full-text search, but is significantly heavier and more complex to self-host than Cabinet.

alternatives.name: Docspell
alternatives.review: Docspell provides robust document organization with OCR and multi-user support but requires more infrastructure setup compared to Cabinet's lightweight approach.

alternatives.name: Nextcloud
alternatives.review: Nextcloud is a full-featured file management and collaboration platform with a massive ecosystem, but is far heavier and overkill for simple document cabinet use cases.

alternatives.name: Teedy
alternatives.review: Teedy offers a polished document management UI with tagging and workflows, but has a larger footprint and steeper learning curve than Cabinet.

alternatives.name: Filestash
alternatives.review: Filestash is similarly lightweight and self-hosted with broad storage backend support, but focuses more on file browsing than structured document management.
